summary: 2026-06-17 12:10:16 PDT — Review of `main...HEAD` found two substantive issues in the edited `/close` flow and one wording inconsistency. The O3 deploy-ordering choice is recorded and is directionally consistent with avoiding an unmerged `install.sh` deploy. I could not verify `bash tests/guard_test.sh` because this read-only sandbox prevented `mktemp`; the guard/test diff itself is empty.

findings.title: Release records can be left on an unmerged PR after a failed merge attempt
findings.claim: Step 5 commits CHANGELOG/BACKLOG release records before invoking the merge path, but the same recipe can still abort afterward, for example when auto-merge is disabled and required checks are detected at lines 56-57, or when the MERGED poll times out at lines 63-68. That contradicts the new BUG-D1 non-regression claim that release records are not speculative and cannot remain on an unmerged branch: a valid merge instruction can still leave a pushed PR branch containing `Done`/changelog records while no merge commit or PR `MERGED` state exists.
findings.suggestion: Move all known merge preflight/abort checks before the release-record commit, and define an explicit cleanup path for any post-commit merge failure or timeout, such as reverting/dropping the release-record commit before stopping or clearly not pushing it until the merge can actually be handed off.

findings.title: Final merged HEAD is no longer gated after the release-record commit
findings.claim: The gate runs in step 3, but step 5 then creates a new release-record commit and `localSha` is taken from that new HEAD. In the direct merge path with no required checks, this untested HEAD is merged immediately, so `/close` no longer guarantees that the exact commit being shipped has passed `testCommand`. Even mechanical CHANGELOG/BACKLOG edits can break markdown or repository checks.
findings.suggestion: After committing the step-5 release records, rerun `testCommand` against that final HEAD before pushing/merging. This does not need a new review round, but it should be the gated commit that `--match-head-commit` protects.

findings.title: CHANGELOG instruction conflicts with the current file shape
findings.claim: The skill says to “turn `[Unreleased]` into” a dated entry, but the changed `CHANGELOG.md` keeps `## [Unreleased]` and adds the dated entry below it. Read literally, future `/close` runs could delete the standing Unreleased section, which is inconsistent with the current Keep a Changelog layout.
findings.suggestion: Reword this to say: add a dated `## [<date>] — <slug> (PR #N)` entry immediately below `## [Unreleased]`, leaving the Unreleased section in place.
